articles.title: Squirrels, spinach doughnuts, and sirens: New England’s best April Fools’ jokes
articles.description:

summary: April Fools' Day featured humorous pranks across New England, including Boston.

Residents engaged in community spirit, sharing laughter and creativity on April 1.

articles.title: New BC women’s basketball coach Kate Popovec-Goss says program’s rebuild ‘will be rooted in relationships’
articles.description:
After coaching stops at La Salle, Northwestern, Colgate, and Northwestern again, she led Bradley from 2022-26.

The post New BC women’s basketball coach Kate Popovec-Goss says program’s rebuild ‘will be rooted in relationships’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kate Popovec-Goss appointed as Boston College women's basketball coach on April 1, 2026.
She previously coached at Bradley from 2022-2026, focusing on building relationships.
Her leadership aims to revitalize the BC women's basketball program for local fans.

articles.title: She’s running Boston for the hospital that saved her daughter’s life
articles.description:
Leslie Wallus is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital.

The post She’s running Boston for the hospital that saved her daughter’s life appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Leslie Wallus will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor Massachusetts General Hospital.

The marathon raises funds for local healthcare, benefiting Boston families in need.

summary: Fransisca Miraningtyas is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds.

articles.title: Westfield man busted in alleged Red Bull SNAP scam at Chicopee smoke shop
articles.description:
The smoke shop employee is accused of steering customers to buy bulk items with SNAP and EBT benefits — and buying them back at a steep discount.

The post Westfield man busted in alleged Red Bull SNAP scam at Chicopee smoke shop appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Chicopee smoke shop employee accused of facilitating SNAP fraud scheme.
Westfield man allegedly bought back bulk items at steep discounts using EBT benefits.
Local residents may face increased scrutiny and restrictions on SNAP transactions.

articles.title: Free speech rally planned outside BU president’s office amid Pride flag dispute
articles.description:

summary: Protest scheduled for Thursday outside Boston University president's office over Pride flag removal.

Local residents and students express concerns about free speech and inclusivity at BU.
